page: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the e2 server family. It is equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system features hyper-threading with 2 threads per core, supported by a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 0.25 MB L2, and 55 MB L3 cache. It includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a highly capable multi-threaded architecture with some single-threaded limitations. The instance demonstrates strong performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests and PassMark CPU encryption tests, placing in the top 25%. However, Geekbench single-core and text processing scores fall into the bottom 25%, and single-threaded compression tasks score in the bottom 10%. Multi-threaded execution, including Geekbench multi-core, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, consistently registers in the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show stable throughput, averaging 98.83 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 44.29 GB/sec for uncached large-block writes.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-standard-32 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized compute or memory-optimized hardware. Its performance profile makes it well-suited for multi-threaded applications such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference. It is less optimal for single-threaded, compression-heavy, or high-performance computing tasks requiring advanced vector extensions like AVX-512, which are not supported on this instance.
description: The Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, utilizing an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. It features 16 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 55 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal strong performance in single-core stress-ng and CPU encryption tests, while multi-threaded workloads like software compilation, database operations, and static web serving perform in the average tier. Conversely, single-threaded compression tasks show weaker performance. This instance provides a balanced, cost-efficient resource profile for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, and medium-scale LLM inference, though it is not suited for tasks requiring AVX-512 instructions or local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-32 is a general-purpose instance with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-32 is a general-purpose VM featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated x86_64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory.
Balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose workloads.
Strong performance in CPU encryption and single-core stress tests.
Average multi-threaded performance for software compilation and database operations.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Best suited for static web serving, application servers, and medium-scale LLM inference.
